Class EventRepository

java.lang.Object
com.example.codebase.EventRepository
public class EventRepository extends Object
Repository class responsible for loading Event data from Firestore.

All queries are executed against AppDatabase.eventsRef. Successfully loaded event lists are cached in AppCache so that subsequent lookups by ID can be served without a network round-trip.

Malformed Firestore documents are silently skipped to prevent a single bad record from crashing the app.

  • Constructor Details

    • EventRepository

      public EventRepository()

  • Method Details

    • loadActiveEvents

      public static void loadActiveEvents(@NonNull EventRepository.EventsCallback callback)
      Loads all active events from Firestore and delivers them via callback.

      An event is considered active if its registration deadline is null (no deadline set) or has not yet passed at the time of the call. Each Firestore document is normalised via EventSchema.normalizeLoadedEvent(com.google.firebase.firestore.DocumentSnapshot); documents that produce a null result or throw an exception are skipped.

      The resulting list is stored in AppCache before being delivered to EventRepository.EventsCallback.onEventsLoaded(List).

      Parameters:
      callback - The EventRepository.EventsCallback to receive the loaded events or an error.

    • loadEventById

      public static void loadEventById(String eventId, @NonNull EventRepository.EventDetailsCallback callback)
      Loads a single Event by its Firestore document ID.

      The AppCache is checked first; if a matching event is found it is returned immediately without a network call. Otherwise, the event document is fetched from Firestore and normalised via EventSchema.normalizeLoadedEvent(com.google.firebase.firestore.DocumentSnapshot).

      Calls EventRepository.EventDetailsCallback.onError(Exception) if:

      • The document does not exist in Firestore.
      • Normalisation throws an exception.
      • The Firestore query itself fails.
      Parameters:
      eventId - The Firestore document ID of the event to load.
      callback - The EventRepository.EventDetailsCallback to receive the loaded event or an error.
